judge_reason: Both agree on the core elements of a hand-drawn diagram with five numbered positions and a connecting line, but they disagree on the specific shapes (ovals/circles vs circles/dash/arrow) and one adds a label while the other does not, without a direct verbatim text contradiction.
original_image_tags: Hand-drawn sketch on plain paper. The sketch shows five positions of an object, numbered 1 through 5, connected by dotted lines to indicate a path of movement. Positions 1, 2, and 3 are drawn as ovals. Position 4 is a thin, dark, horizontal ellipse, as if the object is being viewed edge-on. Position 5 is an arc shape with an arrow pointing away. The path shows the object rising in a staggered manner from 1 to 3, then moving horizontally to the position where it rolls (4), before disappearing along a curved, rolling path at (5).
gpt_mini_desc: *Image: A typed document page with a hand-drawn motion diagram of a black object's path labeled 1 through 5, showing dotted curved movement lines and an arrow, with headings “CONFIDENTIAL” at top and bottom, “COPY” at the upper right, and a typed caption identifying it as a chart referenced in David N. Johnson’s statement.*
